name: Scroll Progress Intent
summary: A continuous 0..1 scroll-progress driver: bind an element's animation/effect to how far a scroll container (or an element's pass through the viewport) has progressed. Owns the reference (scroll() track vs view() element), the axis (block default, exposed), and the range (named animation-range: entry/exit/cover/contain). The opaque `on-scroll` start condition of animation-orchestration becomes a real, driven progress value. Native-first: lowers to CSS Scroll-driven Animations (animation-timeline) where supported, @supports + JS (ScrollTimeline / IntersectionObserver) fallback elsewhere. Homed in webintents per the ratified distributed placement (#1407 / #1175).

reference.description: What the 0..1 progress is measured against. `scroll` tracks a scroll container's own scroll position (the CSS `scroll()` timeline); `view` tracks a subject element's pass through the scrollport (the CSS `view()` timeline). `scroll` drives page-level effects (a reading-progress bar); `view` drives element-relative reveals (parallax, fade-in as a section enters).

axis.description: The scroll axis the progress is read from. Default `block` (the document's block-flow axis — vertical in horizontal writing modes); `inline` for horizontal scrollers; `x` / `y` pin a physical axis regardless of writing mode. Exposed (not fixed) so a horizontal carousel or an RTL surface can drive progress correctly.

range.description: Which slice of the reference's travel maps to 0..1, borrowing the CSS `animation-range` named ranges. `cover` (default for `view`): the whole time the subject overlaps the scrollport. `contain`: only while the subject is fully within it. `entry` / `exit`: just the entering / leaving phase. For `scroll` the range is the container's full scrollable length unless narrowed. Named ranges are the standard vocabulary — never re-invented as pixel offsets.

description:
What this is

A continuous 0..1 driver sourced from scroll position. It does not decide what animates — it produces the progress value that something else is bound to (a progress bar's width, a parallax layer's transform, a fade's opacity). Realizing work for the ratified #1407 placement call.

Reference — scroll() vs view()

  • scroll: progress along a scroll container — page or a nested scroller. The reading-progress-bar case.
  • view: progress of a subject element through the scrollport — the parallax / reveal case, where 0..1 is relative to that element, not the page.

Axis & range

The axis (default block, exposed) picks which scroll axis is read; the range borrows CSS animation-range named ranges (cover / contain / entry / exit) rather than inventing pixel offsets — the official platform vocabulary.

Native-first lowering

Where supported, this lowers to CSS Scroll-driven Animations — animation-timeline: scroll() / view() with animation-range — a compositor-driven, main-thread-free effect with zero JS. Behind an @supports (animation-timeline: scroll()) guard, the fallback drives the same progress with the JS ScrollTimeline polyfill or an IntersectionObserver + scroll handler. The intent is the contract; the timing host (native vs JS) is an impl detail.

Composition

Composes the Motion intent: under prefers-reduced-motion a scroll-driven effect resolves to its static end-state (the progress bar still reports position; the parallax simply doesn't translate). It is the driven realization of animation-orchestration's trigger: on-scroll — that opaque start condition becomes this continuous progress value.
